data.title: Bursa Malaysia Ends Marginally Lower As Late Selling Hits Blue Chips
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia reversed earlier gains to close slightly lower on May 29, weighed down by late selling in blue-chip counters as geopolitical tensions in West Asia kept investors on the defensive.

At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) slipped 1.86 points, or 0.11%, to 1,683.07, compared to the closing of 1,684.93 on May 28.

Broader market indices were mostly firmer despite the benchmark’s decline, with the FBM 70 rising 270.64 points (1.49%) to 18,484.33, FBM Emas gaining 38.84 points (0.31%) to 12,580.12, FBM Shariah adding 53.16 points (0.42%) to 12,604.07, and the F4GBM up 3.01 points (0.3%) to 1,016.14.

Trading activity surged, with turnover jumping to 4.94 billion shares worth RM10.74 billion compared to 3.53 billion shares worth RM4.19 billion in the previous session.

Among the most actively traded stocks, Axiata Group Bhd edged up two sen to RM2, YTL Corporation Bhd added one sen to RM2.05, Mr DIY Group (M) Bhd gained one sen to RM1.58, QL Resources Bhd rose 20 sen to RM3.60, while Nationgate Holdings Bhd climbed 12 sen to 88 sen.

Despite the broader weakness in the index, market participation remained strong, reflecting elevated trading interest amid volatile regional sentiment.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Extends Midday Gains As Buying Lifts Heavyweights
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended its gains at midday on May 29, tracking firmer regional markets as bargain hunting lifted selected heavyweight industrial and financial stocks.

At 12:30pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 5.61 points, or 0.33%, to 1,690.54 from its close of 1,684.93 on May 28.

Broader market indices also advanced, with the FBM 70 climbing 196.74 points (1.08%) to 18,410.43, the FBM Emas adding 64.44 points (0.51%) to 12,605.72, the FBM Shariah rising 84.31 points (0.67%) to 12,635.22 and the F4GBM gaining 5.02 points (0.5%) to 1,018.15.

Market breadth remained positive with 569 gainers outpacing 450 losers, while 492 counters were unchanged. A total of 1,131 counters were untraded and 53 suspended.

Among heavyweight gainers, Nestle (M) Bhd rose RM1.24 to RM96.24, Petronas Dagangan Bhd added 44 sen to RM17.78, Kelington Group Bhd climbed 42 sen to RM7.85, Ajinomoto (M) Bhd gained 32 sen to RM13.60 and QL Resources Bhd advanced 29 sen to RM3.69.

On the losing side, Hong Leong Industries Bhd fell 30 sen to RM18.08, Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd lost 18 sen to RM19.78, KESM Industries Bhd declined 14 sen to RM4.82, Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd shed 14 sen to RM5.37 and Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Bhd slipped 10 sen to RM16.60.

Nationgate Holdings Bhd remained the most actively traded stock, surging 14.5 sen to 90.5 sen with 92.75 million shares changing hands. Zetrix AI Bhd traded flat at 81.5 sen, while SFP Tech Holdings Bhd and Genetec Technology Bhd each gained two sen to 28 sen and 33.5 sen respectively. YTL Corporation Bhd rose seven sen to RM2.11.

Total turnover stood at 1.88 billion shares valued at RM1.86 billion.

data.title: Singapore Stocks Close Higher As STI Tops 5,000 Mark
data.description:
Singapore equities ended higher on May 29, with the benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) rising 0.9% to close at 5,037.86 points.

The gain extended the market’s upward momentum, with investors maintaining a positive outlook amid improving regional sentiment and continued interest in blue-chip stocks.

The STI’s move above the 5,000-point level underscores the strength of Singapore’s equity market, supported by buying across key sectors.

The strong finish reflects growing confidence among investors, helping the benchmark index remain near record levels as market participants continue to monitor global economic and interest-rate developments.

The STI’s 0.9% advance marked a solid session for Singapore stocks, with the benchmark ending the day firmly in positive territory.

data.title: US Stocks End At Record Highs, Cap Strong Week And Month
data.description:
Wall Street closed at fresh record highs on May 29, with all three major indexes posting gains as strong results from Dell Technologies fuelled a rally in technology stocks.

Reuters reported that the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 363.37 points, or 0.72%, to 51,032.34. The S&P 500 gained 16.44 points, or 0.22%, to finish at 7,580.07, while the Nasdaq Composite added 55.15 points, or 0.21%, to close at 26,972.62.

Technology shares led the advance, with Dell surging 32.8% after raising its full-year profit and revenue forecasts. The technology sector climbed 1.87%, supported by gains in chipmakers and major software companies. Microsoft rose 5.4%, while Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Super Micro Computer gained 12.6% and 11.6%, respectively.

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq recorded weekly gains of 1.43% and 2.39%, while the Dow advanced 0.9%. For May, the S&P 500 gained 5.15%, the Nasdaq surged 8.36%, and the Dow added 2.78%.

The S&P 500 notched its ninth consecutive weekly gain, marking its longest winning streak since December 2023, as investor optimism around artificial intelligence and corporate earnings continued to support equities.

Despite weakness in some sectors, including communication services and consumer staples, technology strength helped drive the broader market higher, extending Wall Street’s record-setting run into month-end.

data.title: Bursa Gains In Early Trade Amid Easing Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened higher, tracking Wall Street’s overnight rally after reports that the United States and Iran had agreed to extend a ceasefire and begin negotiations following recent military tensions.

The benchmark FBM KLCI rose 2.58 points or 0.15% to 1,687.51 as at 9.03am after opening at 1,688.18.

Broader market sentiment was also positive, with the FBM Emas Index gaining 28.21 points to 12,569.49 while the FBM 70 Index added 51.59 points to 18,265.28. The FBM Top 100 Index rose 23.10 points to 12,403.58.

Among sectoral indices, Consumer Products and Services led gains, rising 0.41%, followed by the Energy Index which slipped 0.31% amid mixed oil prices overnight.

On Wall Street, the Nasdaq climbed 0.91% to another record close while the S&P 500 rose 0.58%, supported by easing geopolitical concerns in the Middle East and optimism surrounding continued strength in growth-related stocks.

Market sentiment, however, remained cautious as investors continued digesting softer US economic data and persistent inflation pressures, fuelling expectations that the US Federal Reserve could maintain elevated interest rates for longer.

Among actively traded counters on Bursa Malaysia, NationGate Holdings rose one sen to 77 sen with 47.1 million shares traded, while SFP Tech added half-a-sen to 26.5 sen. CBHB advanced two sen to 58 sen.

On the gainers list, Nestle Malaysia rose 90 sen to RM95.90 while KGB climbed 26 sen to RM7.69. Malayan Cement added 20 sen to RM7.09.

Meanwhile, KESM Industries led decliners, falling 16 sen to RM4.80, while YTL Power dropped 12 sen to RM4.03.

Oil prices were mixed overnight with US crude settling at US$88.90 per barrel while Brent crude eased to US$93.71 as investors monitored developments surrounding the Strait of Hormuz and ongoing US-Iran negotiations.

data.title: Tech Stocks Power Market To Winning Month
data.description:
US stocks rose to record highs to cap a banner month as President Trump said he would soon make a decision on a deal with Iran, while Dell’s earnings report sparked the latest rally in the booming AI trade.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.7%, or over 350 points, closing above the 50,000 level for the first time. The benchmark S&P 500 and tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite each added around 0.2%, as the major indexes closed out another winning week amid burgeoning confidence in the AI trade and hopes of easing global tensions.

The S&P 500 notched its ninth week of gains, in its longest winning run since 2023.Since Trump signaled last week that the US is in the “final stages” of talks with Iran, markets have largely remained upbeat. The president’s statement late Friday afternoon that he would soon make a “final determination” on the deal added further fuel to the rally.

Markets are eager for relief on the US-Iran war front as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz exacerbates rapidly rising prices and drums up concerns about the Federal Reserve’s next moves on interest rates.

In corporates, Dell’s results blew past investors’ expectations, lifting its stock as much as 40% higher. The company issued an upbeat outlook, indicating the rapid expansion of data centers amid the AI boom would continue to drive demand for its servers, which run on Nvidia chips.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.7%, while the benchmark S&P 500 and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite added 0.2%. All three indexes rose to record highs.

The S&P 500 notched a ninth consecutive weekly advance, its longest winning streak since 2023.

Yahoo Finance

data.title: Banking And Plantation Decliners Drag Bursa Lower As Selling Pressure Intensifies
data.description:
The stock market movement came under heavier selling pressure on May 28 as banking and plantation heavyweights led the market lower, with decliners comfortably outpacing gainers across the broader market.

Market breadth stayed firmly negative, with 678 losers against 456 gainers, while 550 counters were unchanged, reflecting cautious investor sentiment.

Banking stocks were among the biggest drags, led by Hong Leong Bank Bhd, which slumped 72 sen to RM21.18, while Malayan Banking Bhd fell 44 sen to RM10.50. Plantation-linked counters also weakened, with Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd declining 38 sen to RM19.96 and Batu Kawan Bhd shedding 44 sen to RM19.96. Rounding up the top five decliners were Petronas Dagangan Bhd, which slipped 30 sen to RM17.34.

On the upside, selective buying emerged in consumer and industrial counters. Allianz Malaysia Bhd topped the gainers list, rising 50 sen to RM21.92, while KESM Industries Bhd advanced 47 sen to RM4.96. Ajinomoto (M) Bhd, United Plantations Bhd and Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd also recorded moderate gains.

Overall, the sentiment remained subdued as investors continued rotating out of heavyweight blue chips amid cautious regional market conditions.

data.title: Gainers Lead Bursa Malaysia Trade As Selected Heavyweights Advance
data.description:
The stock market movement ended the trade for the last day of the month on a positive note, with gainers outpacing losers 680 to 512 as selective buying in heavyweight counters lifted overall sentiment.

Among the top gainers, Hong Leong Industries Bhd rose 62 sen to RM19, Ajinomoto (M) Bhd gained 58 sen to RM13.86, United Plantations Bhd added 58 sen to RM31.08, Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Bhd climbed 48 sen to RM17.18, while Petronas Dagangan Bhd advanced 40 sen to RM17.74 on heavy volume.

On the losers’ side, Nestle (M) Bhd led declines, falling RM4 to RM91, followed by Allianz Malaysia Bhd down 40 sen to RM21.52, Malaysia Cement Bhd shedding 35 sen to RM6.54, Hong Leong Bank Bhd slipping 30 sen to RM20.88 and Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd easing 30 sen to RM5.21.

Market breadth remained firmly positive, with 529 counters unchanged, 921 untraded and 51 suspended, reflecting selective participation across the broader market.

data.title: Selling Pressure Deepens On Hang Seng Futures, RHB
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its negative trading bias on the Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) after the contract fell sharply below a key support level amid intensifying selling pressure.

The research house said the HSIF dropped 420 points on Thursday to close at 24,845 points after touching an intraday low of 24,598 points.

According to RHB Research, the formation of a long bearish candlestick and the breach below the 25,100-point support level reinforced the bearish technical setup, signalling that downside momentum remains strong.

Despite the weak sentiment, the research house noted that the HSIF may attempt to stabilise in the near term after forming a long lower shadow near the 24,500-point level, which could indicate interim support.

“If the index rebounds from the support, this will attract fresh buying pressure to lift it towards the resistance of 25,800 points,” the report said.

However, RHB Research said it would continue maintaining a bearish trading outlook unless the HSIF manages to close above the 26,600-point threshold.

The research house also advised traders to maintain short positions initiated on Feb 26 at 26,367 points, while keeping the stop-loss level at 26,600 points to manage trading risks.

Following the recent bearish breakout, RHB Research pegged immediate support levels at 24,500 points and 24,000 points, while resistance levels are seen at 25,800 points and 26,600 points.

data.title: SGX Opens Firm As Investors Track US-Iran Truce Talks
data.description:
Singapore shares opened higher on Friday morning, tracking overnight gains on Wall Street after reports that the United States and Iran had agreed to extend a ceasefire and begin negotiations following recent military tensions.

The Straits Times Index (STI) rose 31.72 points or 0.64% to 5,020.91 as at 9.08am, according to SGX data.

Market breadth was firmly positive with 150 gainers against 49 decliners, while total trading volume stood at 93.12 million shares worth S$151.18 million in early trade.

Investor sentiment improved after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq closed at fresh record highs overnight, helped by easing geopolitical concerns in the Middle East. The Nasdaq gained 0.91% while the S&P 500 rose 0.58%, with investors returning to growth-oriented assets despite lingering concerns over inflation and interest rates.

Analysts said markets were also reacting to hopes that tensions surrounding the Strait of Hormuz may stabilise following reports of renewed talks between Washington and Tehran.

Across SGX derivatives, trading activity remained active with total volume reaching 14,370 contracts in early trade.

Among regional futures, Nikkei 225 Index Futures for June traded at 65,730 while MSCI Singapore Index Futures stood at 459.60. SGX FTSE Taiwan Index Futures were at 3,886.25.

Oil prices remained mixed overnight as investors weighed the possibility of an extended truce against ongoing concerns surrounding global energy supply routes. US crude settled slightly higher at US$88.90 per barrel while Brent crude eased to US$93.71.

Markets are also watching upcoming US inflation data closely as expectations grow that the Federal Reserve could keep rates elevated for longer amid persistent price pressures.

data.title: Yen Nears Critical 160 Mark Again As Japan Signals Readiness To Intervene
data.description:
Japan’s yen is once again hovering near levels that previously triggered government intervention, with traders closely watching whether Tokyo will step back into the currency market as pressure on the Japanese currency intensifies.

According to Reuters, the yen weakened to 159.65 against the US dollar on Thursday, its softest level since late April when Japanese authorities were suspected to have spent around US$63 billion buying yen to stabilise the currency. Despite holding roughly US$1 trillion in foreign reserves, analysts believe Japan’s room for repeated large-scale intervention remains limited.

Mitsubishi UFJ Morgan Stanley Securities chief foreign exchange strategist Daisaku Ueno said the “war of nerves” between authorities and currency speculators is likely to continue as selling pressure on the yen shows little sign of easing. Goldman Sachs economist Yuriko Tanaka estimated Japan still has enough reserves for around 30 more intervention rounds, though analysts warned aggressively selling US Treasury holdings could strain ties with Washington.

Reuters reported that Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama declined to confirm whether intervention had taken place, but reiterated that authorities stood ready to take “decisive action” if necessary.

The yen has come under renewed pressure partly due to soaring energy prices linked to the ongoing Middle East conflict, which has worsened Japan’s import bill. Analysts also pointed to the Bank of Japan’s cautious stance on interest rate hikes and expectations of further fiscal stimulus under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi as factors weighing on the currency.

Market participants now expect Japan could intervene again before the yen breaches the 162 per dollar level.

data.title: Stock Picks: Allianz Malaysia And Guan Chong
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Allianz Malaysia and Guan Chong as stocks with further upside potential following bullish technical breakouts.

The research house said Allianz Malaysia could continue its upward trajectory after breaking above the RM21.80 resistance level, signalling strong positive momentum in the near term.

According to RHB Research, the stock has remained above its 21-day simple moving average line, indicating that the short-term bullish trend is still intact.

The research house expects Allianz Malaysia to potentially climb towards the next resistance levels of RM22.80 and RM23.60 if buying momentum continues. However, it noted that a decline below the RM21 support level could invalidate the bullish setup and trigger a correction phase.

Meanwhile, RHB Research said Guan Chong is also poised to extend its gains after staging a strong breakout above the RM0.965 resistance level.

The counter recently formed a long bullish candlestick pattern, which the research house said reflected sustained buying interest and firm upward momentum.

Should the momentum persist, Guan Chong could rise further towards RM1.02 before testing the RM1.07 level, according to the report.

RHB Research added that a fall below the RM0.92 support level would weaken the bullish outlook and may result in a renewed correction for the stock.

data.title: Asian Shares Tumble As Gulf Conflict Escalates And Oil Surges
data.description:
Asian markets retreated on Thursday as renewed tensions in the Gulf rattled investors, sending oil prices sharply higher while adding fresh uncertainty to the global inflation and interest rate outlook.

Brent crude climbed 3.6% to US$97.71 a barrel while US crude rose 3.8% to US$92.05 after reports of fresh US military strikes targeting Iranian drone operations and claims from Tehran that it had attacked a US air base in Kuwait.

The latest developments dampened earlier optimism surrounding a possible ceasefire agreement and raised concerns over disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil shipping route.

Madison Cartwright, senior geo economics analyst at CBA, said: “Over the next two weeks, we expect either a deal for a new ceasefire, or the current ceasefire will have collapsed with active hostilities resuming.”

He added that there was a 70% probability of a deal eventually being reached but warned uncertainty surrounding the strait remained high.

“Insurance through the strait has become prohibitively expensive and it’s unclear how and at what price insurance will be made available,” he said. “It is also not clear if Iran will charge a toll, or a toll by another name.”

The spike in oil prices pressured regional equities, with Japan’s Nikkei falling 1.4% while South Korean stocks slid 3.2%. MSCI’s broadest index of Asia Pacific shares outside Japan dropped 2.1%.

US Treasury yields also moved higher, with the 10 year yield climbing four basis points to 4.526% as investors worried elevated oil prices could worsen inflation pressures.

Attention is now shifting towards upcoming US personal consumption expenditures data, the Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation gauge. Markets are expecting headline inflation to rise to 3.8% while core inflation is forecast at 3.3%, well above the Fed’s 2% target.

Analysts at NAB said: “With inflation well above target but the growth impact of the conflict still uncertain, the Fed faces genuine two-sided risk.”

The changing interest rate outlook also supported the US dollar, which strengthened against major currencies including the Japanese yen.

data.title: Possible US-Iran Truce Boosts Ringgit Against Dollar
data.description:
The ringgit opened higher against the greenback on Friday amid news about the possible United States-Iran truce, which is expected to have a positive impact on the local note, said an analyst to Bernama. 

At 8 am, the local currency rose to 3.9755/9815 against the greenback from Thursday’s close of 3.9770/9805.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid expects the ringgit-US dollar pair to trade in a RM3.95 to RM3.97 range today.

“News on the 60-day US-Iran truce has led to a decline in Brent crude oil prices by 1.04 per cent to US$93.31 per barrel. The news (about the truce) is unconfirmed, but the market has reacted positively. 

“This suggests that the investing community is fatigued with the nature of the unstable negotiations,” he told Bernama. 

At the same time, the US personal consumption expenditures (PCE) inflation rate accelerated to 3.8 per cent in April from 3.5 per cent the month before, indicating that higher oil prices have eroded Americans’ purchasing power. 

Meanwhile, initial jobless claims also climbed to 215,000 last week from 210,000 previously. It has been on the uptrend since April 30. 

As a result, the US Dollar Index (DXY) was down by 0.21 per cent to 98.997 points.

Back home, the ringgit traded lower against a basket of major currencies. 

It fell against the British pound to 5.3447/3527 from 5.3272/3319 at Thursday’s close, declined against the euro to 4.6323/6392 from 4.6181/6222, and eased versus the Japanese yen to 2.4961/5000 from yesterday’s 2.4947/4970. 

The local note slid versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1151/1201 from 3.1085/1115 at Thursday’s close, and slipped against the Thai baht to 12.2083/2338  from 12.1613/1773 previously. 

It was little changed against the Indonesian rupiah at 222.7/223.2 versus 222.8/223.1 previously and was flat vis-a-vis the the Philippine peso at 6.45/6.46.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum Slide As Crypto Sell-Off Deepens
data.description:
Bitcoin and Ethereum extended their slide on May 29 as risk-off sentiment gripped crypto markets amid geopolitical tensions and heavy institutional outflows, with investors retreating from digital assets despite easing volatility in broader equities.

Bitcoin hovered near the US$73,000 level after falling more than 3% over the past 24 hours, while Ethereum slipped below the key US$2,000 psychological mark, deepening losses for the second-largest cryptocurrency.

Analysts attributed the weakness to escalating US-Iran tensions, persistent ETF outflows and rising concerns over global interest rates.

The broader crypto market also came under pressure, with liquidations topping US$700 million in a single day as leveraged traders were caught in the sell-off. Ethereum’s decline has been sharper than Bitcoin’s in recent weeks, reflecting weaker sentiment toward altcoins and continued rotation into safer assets.

Market watchers said Bitcoin’s ability to hold above the US$70,000 range will be closely monitored, while Ethereum faces growing pressure after breaching critical support near US$2,000.

data.title: South Korean Stocks Surge To Record High As AI Rally Powers Kospi
data.description:
South Korean stocks rebounded sharply on May 29, with the benchmark Kospi soaring 3.55% to close at a record 8,476.15, driven by strong gains in artificial intelligence (AI)-related shares and renewed optimism over a potential cease-fire between the US and Iran.

The index hit an intraday all-time high of 8,615.09 before trimming some gains by the close, resuming its record-setting rally after a brief pullback in the previous session.

Technology and AI-linked stocks led the advance. Samsung Electronics climbed 5.84%, while SK hynix gained 1.92%. LG Electronics surged nearly 30% and internet giant Naver jumped 14.15% amid reports that Nvidia founder Jensen Huang is set to visit South Korea next week.

Automakers also rallied, with Hyundai Motor rising 6.79% and Hyundai Mobis advancing 11.95%.

Investor sentiment was further supported by reports that Washington and Tehran had agreed to extend a cease-fire for 60 days and resume talks on Iran’s nuclear programme, easing geopolitical concerns.

Institutional investors were strong net buyers, helping offset selling by foreign and retail investors, as the Kospi extended its climb above the 8,000-point milestone reached earlier this week.

data.title: Wall Street Hits Fresh Highs As Oil Eases On US-Iran Truce Talks
data.description:
Wall Street closed higher on Thursday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posting fresh record highs after reports emerged that the United States and Iran had agreed to extend a ceasefire and begin negotiations following recent military exchanges.

The easing geopolitical tension helped calm investors after days of uncertainty surrounding the Middle East conflict, although concerns over inflation and interest rates continued to linger across financial markets.

The Nasdaq jumped 0.91% to 26,917.47, while the S&P 500 gained 0.58% to finish at 7,563.78. The Dow Jones Industrial Average also edged up 0.05% to 50,669.77.

European markets, however, remained under pressure despite recovering from heavier losses earlier in the session, as traders continued weighing geopolitical risks and slowing economic growth signals.

“There’s certainly been a degree of day-to-day volatility in markets as a result of geopolitical events,” said Bill Merz, head of Capital Market Research at U.S. Bank Wealth Management.

“But in spite of all the negative news and uncertainty around the Middle East, we’re seeing this intersection of fundamentals and market signals really sending a consistent message that growth is strong, growth-oriented assets continue to perform, and we’re at all-time highs,” he added.

Oil prices were mixed following reports of a possible extension to the truce. US crude settled 0.25% higher at US$88.90 per barrel, while Brent crude slipped 0.62% to US$93.71 per barrel amid uncertainty surrounding shipping activity through the Strait of Hormuz.

Investors were also digesting weaker-than-expected US economic data, including softer first-quarter GDP growth and declining business equipment orders, while inflation pressures continued to build.

Analysts said the combination of slowing growth and rising prices has complicated the US Federal Reserve’s policy outlook, increasing expectations that interest rates could stay elevated for longer or potentially rise again.

Gold prices rebounded 0.9% to US$4,497.35 an ounce, while the US dollar weakened slightly against major currencies. Bitcoin also fell 2.47% to US$73,306.56 as investors turned cautious ahead of key inflation data.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks End Higher As Hang Seng Extends Gains
data.description:
Hong Kong equities closed higher on May 29, with the benchmark Hang Seng Index advancing 0.7% to finish at 25,182.39 points.

The positive performance reflected continued investor confidence in the market, with buying interest supporting gains across key sectors. The rise pushed the Hang Seng Index further above the 25,000-point level, reinforcing its recent upward trend.

Market sentiment remained constructive as investors assessed regional economic developments and broader market conditions, helping sustain momentum in Hong Kong stocks.

The Hang Seng’s 0.7% gain marked another solid session for the market, with the benchmark ending the day comfortably in positive territory and near multi-year highs.
